{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01M3T4U3p5uaTFJHk93mDPs4","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"8f52cf91-da5d-41f5-810a-4a740cd5788c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:00:07.982Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FhCbBLq9ScfSb15ECzub9C","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    33→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    34→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    35→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    36→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    37→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    38→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    39→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    40→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    41→\n    42→## Deployment\n    43→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    44→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    45→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    46→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    47→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    48→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    49→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    50→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    51→\n    52→## React + SQLite\n    53→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    54→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    55→\n    56→## Security\n    57→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    58→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    59→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    60→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    61→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    62→\n    63→## Working Directory\n    64→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    65→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    66→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    67→\n    68→## Security\n    69→- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n    70→- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n    71→- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n    72→- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n    73→- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n    74→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"0cfda78b-7fad-4703-b230-587c98f83648","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:00:08.453Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n\n## Security\n- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n","numLines":74,"startLine":1,"totalLines":74}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BMv9uR6sU6ThnFL4i5ZubD","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-24 08:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 11: focused on data integrity, resource cleanup, and notification logic. Found and fixed 3 bugs. Created issue #96, implemented fixes in PR #97, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 198 tests pass\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo (different project), Google Workspace promo\n    12→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    13→- **PAT status**: Still valid, added reminder comment on #54\n    14→\n    15→## Work Completed\n    16→\n    17→### Issue #96: Code review round 11 (PR #97 — merged + deployed)\n    18→\n    19→3 bugs fixed:\n    20→\n    21→1. **Stale `max_group_size` in approve transaction** (CRITICAL) — Applications route read `max_group_size` outside the transaction closure but used it inside. A concurrent PATCH to the listing could change the value between the outer read and the transaction execution, allowing one extra member past the cap. Fixed by re-reading `max_group_size` inside the transaction, matching the pattern already used in the join route.\n    22→\n    23→2. **Missing `useEffect` cleanup on HomePage** (IMPORTANT) — `debounceRef` and `abortRef` were not cleaned up on unmount. Navigating away during a debounced search left orphaned timers and in-flight fetches that could call setState after unmount. Added cleanup effect to clear timeout and abort controller.\n    24→\n    25→3. **Duplicate author notifications on group fill** (IMPORTANT) — When the last person joined, the author received both a `new_member` notification and a `group_full` notification. Now skips the `new_member` notification when the group fills, since `group_full` is more informative and already implies the join event.\n    26→\n    27→All 198 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n    28→\n    29→## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n    30→- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints — would need schema migration; no user deletion endpoint exists yet\n    31→- `reading_pace` LIKE filter vs free-text field inconsistency — cosmetic, not causing data issues\n    32→- `notifyGroupFull` depends on author being in `listing_members` — fragile invariant but holds today\n    33→\n    34→## Issues Monitored\n    35→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human. Added reminder comment.\n    36→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    37→\n    38→## Next Session Priority\n    39→1. Check production health\n    40→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    41→3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n    42→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"131224f3-5637-48e6-ab18-e0224c7a8c87","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:00:14.939Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-0800.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 08:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 11: focused on data integrity, resource cleanup, and notification logic. Found and fixed 3 bugs. Created issue #96, implemented fixes in PR #97, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 198 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo (different project), Google Workspace promo\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n- **PAT status**: Still valid, added reminder comment on #54\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #96: Code review round 11 (PR #97 — merged + deployed)\n\n3 bugs fixed:\n\n1. **Stale `max_group_size` in approve transaction** (CRITICAL) — Applications route read `max_group_size` outside the transaction closure but used it inside. A concurrent PATCH to the listing could change the value between the outer read and the transaction execution, allowing one extra member past the cap. Fixed by re-reading `max_group_size` inside the transaction, matching the pattern already used in the join route.\n\n2. **Missing `useEffect` cleanup on HomePage** (IMPORTANT) — `debounceRef` and `abortRef` were not cleaned up on unmount. Navigating away during a debounced search left orphaned timers and in-flight fetches that could call setState after unmount. Added cleanup effect to clear timeout and abort controller.\n\n3. **Duplicate author notifications on group fill** (IMPORTANT) — When the last person joined, the author received both a `new_member` notification and a `group_full` notification. Now skips the `new_member` notification when the group fills, since `group_full` is more informative and already implies the join event.\n\nAll 198 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints — would need schema migration; no user deletion endpoint exists yet\n- `reading_pace` LIKE filter vs free-text field inconsistency — cosmetic, not causing data issues\n- `notifyGroupFull` depends on author being in `listing_members` — fragile invariant but holds today\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human. Added reminder comment.\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":42,"startLine":1,"totalLines":42}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HWgGGfPmecetmfU96Gbkhr","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Registered Platforms\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub\n     4→- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     5→- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n     6→- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n     7→\n     8→## Production Deployment\n     9→- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n    10→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    11→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n    12→- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n    13→- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n    14→- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n    15→- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n    16→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"bfee8d11-112d-47ac-ae7e-b41791b752b6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:00:15.382Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/platforms.md","content":"# Registered Platforms\n\n## GitHub\n- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n\n## Production Deployment\n- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n","numLines":16,"startLine":1,"totalLines":16}}}

 7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→ 69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT auto-telegram feature\n   120→try {\n   121→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n   122→} catch {\n   123→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   131→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   132→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n   133→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n   134→  );\n   135→\n   136→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n   137→    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n   138→    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   139→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n   140→  );\n   141→\n   142→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_user_links (\n   143→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   144→    telegram_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   145→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   146→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id),\n   147→    UNIQUE (telegram_user_id),\n   148→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   149→  );\n   150→`);\n   151→\n   152→// Rating and Reputation system\n   153→db.exec(`\n   154→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n   155→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n   156→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   157→    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   158→    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   159→    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n   160→    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   161→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   162→    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n   163→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n   164→    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n   165→    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   166→  );\n   167→`);\n   168→\n   169→// Performance indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n   170→db.exec(`\n   171→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\n   172→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\n   173→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\n   174→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n   175→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_applications_listing_status ON listing_applications(listing_id, status);\n   176→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread ON notifications(user_id, is_read);\n   177→  CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pending_telegram_chat_id ON pending_telegram_groups(telegram_chat_id);\n   178→`);\n   179→\n   180→export default db;\n   181→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"978c0345-e2c9-4234-9cc4-aa1605dd0ba6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:01:06.651Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01EAtwxRiLaZzMAy6ADKu8Dq","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→\n     4→export interface SessionData {\n     5→  userId?: number;\n     6→  email?: string;\n     7→  displayName?: string;\n     8→}\n     9→\n    10→const DEV_SECRET =\n    11→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    12→\n    13→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    14→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    15→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    16→    throw new Error(\n    17→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→  return {\n    21→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    22→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    23→    cookieOptions: {\n    24→      httpOnly: true,\n    25→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n    26→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    27→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    28→    },\n    29→  };\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→export async function getSession() {\n    33→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    34→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    35→}\n    36→\n    37→export async function requireAuth() {\n    38→  const session = await getSession();\n    39→  if (!session.userId) {\n    40→    return null;\n    41→  }\n    42→  return session;\n    43→}\n    44→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"8aff46af-4a0f-4509-8b45-373760767211","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:01:06.895Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01KP4C1c8fx65TwSNcRULCUR","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01M12UJ2MEenoVEHGtw9TpmT","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2958,"cache_read_input_tokens":8522,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2958,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":43,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"6deb1173-13e1-479e-830d-6a2279f48164"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01M12UJ2MEenoVEHGtw9TpmT","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→import { escapeHtmlForEmail } from \"./crypto-utils\";\n     4→\n     5→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     6→  const user = db\n     7→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     8→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     9→  return user?.email || null;\n    10→}\n    11→\n    12→export function createNotification(\n    13→  userId: number,\n    14→  listingId: number | null,\n    15→  type: string,\n    16→  message: string\n    17→) {\n    18→  db.prepare(\n    19→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    20→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    21→\n    22→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    23→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    24→  if (email) {\n    25→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    26→    // HTML-escape the message to prevent injection via user-controlled content\n    27→    // (display names, book titles) embedded in notification messages\n    28→    const htmlBody = `<p>${escapeHtmlForEmail(message)}</p>`;\n    29→    sendEmail(email, subject, message, htmlBody).catch(() => {\n    30→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    31→    });\n    32→  }\n    33→}\n    34→\n    35→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    36→  switch (type) {\n    37→    case \"new_member\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    39→    case \"group_full\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    41→    case \"application_received\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    43→    case \"application_approved\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    45→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    47→    case \"rating_received\":\n    48→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    49→    case \"listing_deleted\":\n    50→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been cancelled\";\n    51→    case \"listing_updated\":\n    52→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been updated\";\n    53→    case \"telegram_ready\":\n    54→      return \"Bookmate: Your Telegram reading group is ready!\";\n    55→    case \"discord_ready\":\n    56→      return \"Bookmate: Your Discord channel is ready!\";\n    57→    default:\n    58→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    59→  }\n    60→}\n    61→\n    62→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    63→  listingId: number,\n    64→  joinerName: string\n    65→) {\n    66→  const listing = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    69→    )\n    70→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    71→\n    72→  if (listing) {\n    73→    createNotification(\n    74→      listing.author_id,\n    75→      listingId,\n    76→      \"new_member\",\n    77→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    78→    );\n    79→  }\n    80→}\n    81→\n    82→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    83→  listingId: number,\n    84→  applicantName: string\n    85→) {\n    86→  const listing = db\n    87→    .prepare(\n    88→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    89→    )\n    90→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    91→\n    92→  if (listing) {\n    93→    createNotification(\n    94→      listing.author_id,\n    95→      listingId,\n    96→      \"application_received\",\n    97→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    98→    );\n    99→  }\n   100→}\n   101→\n   102→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n   103→  userId: number,\n   104→  listingId: number,\n   105→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n   106→) {\n   107→  const listing = db\n   108→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   109→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   110→\n   111→  if (listing) {\n   112→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   113→    const message =\n   114→      decision === \"approved\"\n   115→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   116→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   117→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   118→  }\n   119→}\n   120→\n   121→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   122→  const listing = db\n   123→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   124→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string; author_id: number } | undefined;\n   125→  // Include all members (including the author) — author needs to know group is full to create the chat\n   126→  const members = db\n   127→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   128→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   129→\n   130→  if (listing) {\n   131→    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n   132→    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n   133→      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n   134→      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   135→    const message = botConfigured\n   136→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n   137→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n   138→\n   139→    for (const member of members) {\n   140→      createNotification(\n   141→        member.user_id,\n   142→        listingId,\n   143→        \"group_full\",\n   144→        message\n   145→      );\n   146→    }\n   147→  }\n   148→}\n   149→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"294408d2-c430-4881-b959-672d14b8e490","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:01:07.417Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01KP4C1c8fx65TwSNcRULCUR","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01DsgJaexRhA53zJs9BoN4X5","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/rate-limit.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2958,"cache_read_input_tokens":8522,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2958,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":43,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"c7bbd6b6-4e32-4217-85ee-84a400866e16"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DsgJaexRhA53zJs9BoN4X5","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Simple in-memory rate limiter for auth endpoints.\n     3→ * Uses a sliding window approach keyed by identifier (IP + route).\n     4→ * Not shared across cluster workers — acceptable for SQLite single-process setup.\n     5→ */\n     6→\n     7→interface RateLimitEntry {\n     8→  timestamps: number[];\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→const store = new Map<string, RateLimitEntry>();\n    12→\n    13→// Clean up expired entries every 5 minutes\n    14→// Guard against multiple intervals on Next.js hot reload\n    15→const CLEANUP_INTERVAL = 5 * 60 * 1000;\n    16→const globalObj = globalThis as unknown as { __rateLimitCleanup?: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> };\n    17→if (!globalObj.__rateLimitCleanup) {\n    18→  globalObj.__rateLimitCleanup = setInterval(() => {\n    19→    const now = Date.now();\n    20→    for (const [key, entry] of store) {\n    21→      entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < 15 * 60 * 1000);\n    22→      if (entry.timestamps.length === 0) {\n    23→        store.delete(key);\n    24→      }\n    25→    }\n    26→  }, CLEANUP_INTERVAL);\n    27→}\n    28→\n    29→/**\n    30→ * Check if a request should be rate limited.\n    31→ * @param key - Unique identifier (e.g., IP + email for login, IP for register)\n    32→ * @param maxAttempts - Maximum attempts allowed in the window\n    33→ * @param windowMs - Time window in milliseconds (default: 15 minutes)\n    34→ * @returns Object with allowed boolean and retryAfter seconds (0 if allowed)\n    35→ */\n    36→export function checkRateLimit(\n    37→  key: string,\n    38→  maxAttempts: number = 5,\n    39→  windowMs: number = 15 * 60 * 1000\n    40→): { allowed: boolean; retryAfter: number } {\n    41→  const now = Date.now();\n    42→  let entry = store.get(key);\n    43→\n    44→  if (!entry) {\n    45→    // Prevent unbounded memory growth under attack\n    46→    if (store.size > 50_000) {\n    47→      return { allowed: false, retryAfter: 60 };\n    48→    }\n    49→    entry = { timestamps: [] };\n    50→    store.set(key, entry);\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  // Remove expired timestamps\n    54→  entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < windowMs);\n    55→\n    56→  if (entry.timestamps.length >= maxAttempts) {\n    57→    const oldestInWindow = entry.timestamps[0];\n    58→    const retryAfter = Math.ceil((oldestInWindow + windowMs - now) / 1000);\n    59→    return { allowed: false, retryAfter };\n    60→  }\n    61→\n    62→  entry.timestamps.push(now);\n    63→  return { allowed: true, retryAfter: 0 };\n    64→}\n    65→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"9191f493-e367-4923-b2da-37cd293a5784","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:01:07.457Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SwWVKH2vokyMFD5MEYYffM","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n    30→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    42→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Check if already a member\n    46→  const existing = db\n    47→    .prepare(\n    48→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    )\n    50→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    51→\n    52→  if (existing) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Check if user has a pending application (e.g., requires_approval was toggled off)\n    57→  const pendingApp = db\n    58→    .prepare(\n    59→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    60→    )\n    61→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    62→\n    63→  if (pendingApp) {\n    64→    return NextResponse.json(\n    65→      { error: \"You already have a pending application for this group\" },\n    66→      { status: 400 }\n    67→    );\n    68→  }\n    69→\n    70→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    71→  // Re-read max_group_size inside the transaction to avoid stale data from concurrent PATCH\n    72→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    73→    const freshListing = db\n    74→      .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    75→      .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\n    76→\n    77→    if (!freshListing) {\n    78→      return { error: \"Listing not found\", count: 0, maxSize: 0 };\n    79→    }\n    80→\n    81→    const { count } = db\n    82→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    83→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    84→\n    85→    if (count >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    86→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count, maxSize: freshListing.max_group_size };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    db.prepare(\n    90→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    91→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    92→\n    93→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    94→    if (newCount >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    95→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    96→    }\n    97→\n    98→    return { error: null, count: newCount, maxSize: freshListing.max_group_size };\n    99→  });\n   100→\n   101→  const result = joinTransaction();\n   102→\n   103→  if (result.error) {\n   104→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n   105→  }\n   106→\n   107→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   108→  // When group just filled, skip new_member notification for the author\n   109→  // since they'll receive the more important group_full notification instead\n   110→  if (result.count === result.maxSize) {\n   111→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n   112→  } else {\n   113→    notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   114→  }\n   115→\n   116→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n   117→}\n   118→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"35ec0783-2a56-4515-96b4-0130b356301a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:01:10.268Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01ExAJQ4NgYYiCE9mw19zWur","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Vwt9PJuFZhjihpiunPJ4AB","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5819,"cache_read_input_tokens":11480,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5819,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"e5c65e77-014d-428a-bdf0-35ed75a06ad0"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Vwt9PJuFZhjihpiunPJ4AB","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationReceived } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json(\n    31→      { error: \"You cannot apply to your own listing\" },\n    32→      { status: 400 }\n    33→    );\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (!listing.requires_approval) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"This group does not require approval. Join directly.\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    44→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    45→  }\n    46→\n    47→  // Wrap membership check + application in a transaction to prevent TOCTOU races\n    48→  const applyTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    49→    // Check if already a member\n    50→    const isMember = db\n    51→      .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    52→      .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    53→\n    54→    if (isMember) {\n    55→      return { error: \"You are already a member\", status: 400 };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    // Re-check is_full inside transaction\n    59→    const currentListing = db\n    60→      .prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    61→      .get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n    62→\n    63→    if (currentListing?.is_full) {\n    64→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", status: 400 };\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    // Check if already applied (pending)\n    68→    const existingApp = db\n    69→      .prepare(\n    70→        \"SELECT id, status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    71→      )\n    72→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { id: number; status: string } | undefined;\n    73→\n    74→    if (existingApp) {\n    75→      if (existingApp.status === \"pending\") {\n    76→        return { error: \"You have already applied\", status: 400 };\n    77→      }\n    78→      if (existingApp.status === \"approved\") {\n    79→        return { error: \"You are already approved\", status: 400 };\n    80→      }\n    81→      // If rejected, allow re-application by updating status back to pending\n    82→      db.prepare(\n    83→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'pending', decided_at = NULL, applied_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    84→      ).run(existingApp.id);\n    85→\n    86→      return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    // Insert new application\n    90→    db.prepare(\n    91→      \"INSERT INTO listing_applications (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    92→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    93→\n    94→    return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    95→  });\n    96→\n    97→  const result = applyTransaction();\n    98→\n    99→  if (result.error) {\n   100→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: result.status });\n   101→  }\n   102→\n   103→  // Notify outside transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   104→  notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   105→\n   106→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   107→}\n   108→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"1af61ed3-fce3-4598-93bc-1ab789954c2e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:01:10.692Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"adc0ac7b873f9ff45","tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","description":"Reading app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":17583,"tool_uses":9,"duration_ms":11277},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"00057eca-72cb-47ca-857d-9e5e26396cd5","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01ExAJQ4NgYYiCE9mw19zWur","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_015kuvp5gTJ5VPQEvrFwaNv5","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5819,"cache_read_input_tokens":11480,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5819,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"e080e3c1-096f-4fef-a2d7-628c65f88911"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015kuvp5gTJ5VPQEvrFwaNv5","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function PATCH(\n     7→  req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const body = await req.json();\n    34→  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n    35→\n    36→  if (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n    44→  const application = db\n    45→    .prepare(\n    46→      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    47→    )\n    48→    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    49→\n    50→  if (!application) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n    53→      { status: 404 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n    58→\n    59→  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    60→    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    61→    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    62→      // Re-read max_group_size inside transaction to avoid stale data from concurrent PATCH\n    63→      const freshListing = db\n    64→        .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    65→        .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\n    66→\n    67→      if (!freshListing) {\n    68→        throw new Error(\"Listing not found\");\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      // Check if group is already full\n    72→      const { count } = db\n    73→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    74→        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    75→\n    76→      if (count >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    77→        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n    78→      }\n    79→\n    80→      // Update application status\n    81→      db.prepare(\n    82→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    83→      ).run(applicationId);\n    84→\n    85→      // Add to members\n    86→      db.prepare(\n    87→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    88→      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n    89→\n    90→      // Check if group is now full\n    91→      const newCount = count + 1;\n    92→      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n    93→      if (newCount >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    94→        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    95→\n    96→        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n    97→        rejectedApps = db\n    98→          .prepare(\n    99→            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n   100→          )\n   101→          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n   102→\n   103→        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n   104→          db.prepare(\n   105→            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   106→          ).run(app.id);\n   107→        }\n   108→\n   109→        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n   110→      }\n   111→      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n   112→    });\n   113→\n   114→    try {\n   115→      const result = approveTransaction();\n   116→\n   117→      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n   118→      try {\n   119→        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n   120→\n   121→        if (result.isFull) {\n   122→          notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n   123→          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n   124→            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   125→          }\n   126→        }\n   127→      } catch (notifErr) {\n   128→        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n   129→      }\n   130→\n   131→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   132→    } catch (err) {\n   133→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n   134→      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n   135→    }\n   136→  } else {\n   137→    // Reject\n   138→    db.prepare(\n   139→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   140→    ).run(applicationId);\n   141→\n   142→    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   143→\n   144→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   145→  }\n   146→}\n   147→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"b6575da8-a9c5-4ae9-b243-acb290403977","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:01:11.164Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"adc0ac7b873f9ff45","tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","description":"Reading app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":17619,"tool_uses":10,"duration_ms":11427},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"f04faf58-36a1-40f5-94d9-5bccae5c0b0e","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01ExAJQ4NgYYiCE9mw19zWur","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_015uGW6D45CB1F7n5c5DZd3U","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5819,"cache_read_input_tokens":11480,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5819,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"9dd2322e-f24a-4e52-bcc4-3b99545084eb"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015uGW6D45CB1F7n5c5DZd3U","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession, requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→import { isBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     6→import { isBotConfigured as isDiscordBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     7→\n     8→export async function GET(\n     9→  _req: NextRequest,\n    10→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    11→) {\n    12→  const { id } = await params;\n    13→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    14→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\n    20→      `SELECT\n    21→        l.*,\n    22→        u.display_name as author_name\n    23→      FROM listings l\n    24→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    25→      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    26→    )\n    27→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    28→\n    29→  if (!listing) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const members = db\n    34→    .prepare(\n    35→      `SELECT u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, lm.joined_at\n    36→       FROM listing_members lm\n    37→       JOIN users u ON lm.user_id = u.id\n    38→       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n    39→       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    40→    )\n    41→    .all(listingId) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n    42→\n    43→  const session = await getSession();\n    44→  const isMember = session.userId\n    45→    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    46→    : false;\n    47→  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    48→    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    49→    : false;\n    50→\n    51→  // PRD: full listings should be invisible to non-participants\n    52→  if (listing.is_full && !isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json(\n    54→      { error: \"This listing is no longer available\" },\n    55→      { status: 404 }\n    56→    );\n    57→  }\n    58→\n    59→  // Check if the current user has an application (regardless of requires_approval,\n    60→  // since the flag may have been toggled after the application was submitted)\n    61→  let hasApplied = false;\n    62→  let applicationStatus = \"\";\n    63→  if (session.userId && !isMember) {\n    64→    const app = db\n    65→      .prepare(\n    66→        \"SELECT status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    67→      )\n    68→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { status: string } | undefined;\n    69→    if (app) {\n    70→      hasApplied = app.status === \"pending\";\n    71→      applicationStatus = app.status;\n    72→    }\n    73→  }\n    74→\n    75→  // Fetch pending applicants if the viewer is the author\n    76→  let pendingApplicants: { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[] = [];\n    77→  if (isAuthor && listing.requires_approval) {\n    78→    pendingApplicants = db\n    79→      .prepare(\n    80→        `SELECT la.id as application_id, u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, la.applied_at\n    81→         FROM listing_applications la\n    82→         JOIN users u ON la.user_id = u.id\n    83→         WHERE la.listing_id = ? AND la.status = 'pending'\n    84→         ORDER BY la.applied_at ASC`\n    85→      )\n    86→      .all(listingId) as { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[];\n    87→  }\n    88→\n    89→  // Strip platform links for non-members to prevent leaking invite URLs\n    90→  const safeListingData = { ...listing };\n    91→  if (!isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    92→    delete safeListingData.telegram_link;\n    93→    delete safeListingData.discord_link;\n    94→  }\n    95→\n    96→  return NextResponse.json({\n    97→    listing: {\n    98→      ...safeListingData,\n    99→      members,\n   100→      memberCount: members.length,\n   101→      isMember,\n   102→      isAuthor,\n   103→      hasApplied,\n   104→      applicationStatus,\n   105→      pendingApplicants,\n   106→      currentUserId: session.userId || null,\n   107→      telegramBotConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n   108→      discordBotConfigured: isDiscordBotConfigured(),\n   109→    },\n   110→  });\n   111→}\n   112→\n   113→export async function PATCH(\n   114→  req: NextRequest,\n   115→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   116→) {\n   117→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   118→  if (!session) {\n   119→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   120→  }\n   121→\n   122→  const { id } = await params;\n   123→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   124→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   125→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   126→  }\n   127→\n   128→  const listing = db\n   129→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   130→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   131→\n   132→  if (!listing) {\n   133→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n   134→  }\n   135→\n   136→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n   137→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can edit this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n   138→  }\n   139→\n   140→  // Cannot edit if a platform link has been shared (group already formed)\n   141→  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n   142→    return NextResponse.json(\n   143→      { error: \"Cannot edit a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   144→      { status: 400 }\n   145→    );\n   146→  }\n   147→\n   148→  try {\n   149→    const body = await req.json();\n   150→    const {\n   151→      language,\n   152→      readingPace,\n   153→      startDate,\n   154→      meetingFormat,\n   155→      maxGroupSize,\n   156→      requiresApproval,\n   157→      platformPreference,\n   158→    } = body;\n   159→\n   160→    // Validate fields that are provided — type check before string operations\n   161→    if (readingPace !== undefined && (typeof readingPace !== \"string\" || !readingPace || readingPace.length > 200)) {\n   162→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Reading pace is required (max 200 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   163→    }\n   164→\n   165→    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n   166→      if (typeof startDate !== \"string\" || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n   167→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   168→      }\n   169→      const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n   170→      if (startDate < today) {\n   171→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Start date cannot be in the past\" }, { status: 400 });\n   172→      }\n   173→    }\n   174→\n   175→    if (meetingFormat !== undefined && (typeof meetingFormat !== \"string\" || ![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat))) {\n   176→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid meeting format\" }, { status: 400 });\n   177→    }\n   178→\n   179→    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n   180→      if (typeof maxGroupSize !== \"number\" && typeof maxGroupSize !== \"string\") {\n   181→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid group size type\" }, { status: 400 });\n   182→      }\n   183→      const size = parseInt(String(maxGroupSize), 10);\n   184→      if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n   185→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" }, { status: 400 });\n   186→      }\n   187→\n   188→      // Cannot reduce below current member count\n   189→      const memberCount = (db\n   190→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   191→        .get(listingId) as { count: number }).count;\n   192→\n   193→      if (size < memberCount) {\n   194→        return NextResponse.json(\n   195→          { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${memberCount})` },\n   196→          { status: 400 }\n   197→        );\n   198→      }\n   199→    }\n   200→\n   201→    if (language !== undefined && (!language || typeof language !== \"string\" || language.length > 100)) {\n   202→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Language is required (max 100 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   203→    }\n   204→\n   205→    if (platformPreference !== undefined && ![\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)) {\n   206→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid platform preference\" }, { status: 400 });\n   207→    }\n   208→\n   209→    // Build dynamic UPDATE\n   210→    const updates: string[] = [];\n   211→    const values: unknown[] = [];\n   212→\n   213→    if (language !== undefined) {\n   214→      updates.push(\"language = ?\");\n   215→      values.push(language);\n   216→    }\n   217→    if (readingPace !== undefined) {\n   218→      updates.push(\"reading_pace = ?\");\n   219→      values.push(readingPace.trim());\n   220→    }\n   221→    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n   222→      updates.push(\"start_date = ?\");\n   223→      values.push(startDate);\n   224→    }\n   225→    if (meetingFormat !== undefined) {\n   226→      updates.push(\"meeting_format = ?\");\n   227→      values.push(meetingFormat);\n   228→    }\n   229→    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n   230→      const newSize = parseInt(String(maxGroupSize), 10);\n   231→      updates.push(\"max_group_size = ?\");\n   232→      values.push(newSize);\n   233→\n   234→      // If increasing group size on a full listing, reopen it\n   235→      const currentMemberCount = (db\n   236→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   237→        .get(listingId) as { count: number }).count;\n   238→      if (newSize > currentMemberCount && listing.is_full) {\n   239→        updates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n   240→        values.push(0);\n   241→      }\n   242→    }\n   243→    if (requiresApproval !== undefined) {\n   244→      updates.push(\"requires_approval = ?\");\n   245→      values.push(requiresApproval ? 1 : 0);\n   246→    }\n   247→    if (platformPreference !== undefined) {\n   248→      updates.push(\"platform_preference = ?\");\n   249→      values.push(platformPreference);\n   250→    }\n   251→\n   252→    if (updates.length === 0) {\n   253→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"No fields to update\" }, { status: 400 });\n   254→    }\n   255→\n   256→    values.push(listingId);\n   257→    db.prepare(`UPDATE listings SET ${updates.join(\", \")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...values);\n   258→\n   259→    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n   260→  } catch (error) {\n   261→    console.error(\"Update listing error:\", error);\n   262→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n   263→  }\n   264→}\n   265→\n   266→export async function DELETE(\n   267→  _req: NextRequest,\n   268→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   269→) {\n   270→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   271→  if (!session) {\n   272→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   273→  }\n   274→\n   275→  const { id } = await params;\n   276→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   277→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   278→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   279→  }\n   280→\n   281→  const listing = db\n   282→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   283→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   284→\n   285→  if (!listing) {\n   286→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n   287→  }\n   288→\n   289→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n   290→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can delete this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n   291→  }\n   292→\n   293→  // Cannot delete if a platform link has been shared\n   294→  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n   295→    return NextResponse.json(\n   296→      { error: \"Cannot delete a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   297→      { status: 400 }\n   298→    );\n   299→  }\n   300→\n   301→  try {\n   302→    // Get members to notify (excluding the author)\n   303→    const members = db\n   304→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != ?\")\n   305→      .all(listingId, session.userId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   306→\n   307→    // Delete in a transaction (notifications sent after, so they aren't wiped)\n   308→    const deleteTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n   309→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   310→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   311→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   312→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM notifications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   313→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM ratings WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   314→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   315→    });\n   316→\n   317→    deleteTransaction();\n   318→\n   319→    // Send deletion notifications after transaction (listing_id is NULL since listing is gone)\n   320→    for (const member of members) {\n   321→      try {\n   322→        createNotification(\n   323→          member.user_id,\n   324→          null,\n   325→          \"listing_deleted\",\n   326→          `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" has been cancelled by the organizer.`\n   327→        );\n   328→      } catch {\n   329→        // Notification failure should not block response\n   330→      }\n   331→    }\n   332→\n   333→    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n   334→  } catch (error) {\n   335→    console.error(\"Delete listing error:\", error);\n   336→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n   337→  }\n   338→}\n   339→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01CFACsxpc4RB8qtGXzsss9n","session_id":"fdc68caf-59cb-4027-98f6-8073dfb4f64d","uuid":"bcc4c903-e5a1-46a4-a3b9-96a7c753e108","timestamp":"2026-03-24T09:01:11.518Z"}
